channel.title: Postcss on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Postcss on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] PostCSS Plugin Error Fix
item.description:
PostCSS Plugin Error

Fix PostCSS plugin errors. Handle plugin loading issues, configuration problems, and CSS processing failures..

What This Error Means

Common error scenarios include:

  • Connection or network failures
  • Invalid configuration or options
  • Resource not found or unavailable
  • Permission or access issues

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Incorrect configuration or missing dependencies
// Cause 2: Network or connection issues
// Cause 3: Invalid input or parameters
// Cause 4: Missing dependencies or resources

How to Fix

Fix 1: Verify configuration and dependencies

// Check configuration values and ensure required dependencies are installed
// Verify the module/package is properly configured for your environment

Fix 2: Add proper error handling

try {
 // Use the module/package with proper error handling
} catch (err) {
 console.error('Error:', err.message);
 // Handle gracefully
}

Fix 3: Add retry and timeout logic

// For network operations, add timeout and retry logic
// For file operations, check existence before accessing

Examples

// Common error handling pattern
try {
 // Operation that may fail
} catch (err) {
 if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
 console.error('Connection refused - check if service is running');
 } else if (err.code === 'ETIMEDOUT') {
 console.error('Operation timed out');
 }
}

Related Errors

  • ECONNREFUSED — connection refused
  • ETIMEDOUT — timeout
  • ENOENT — file not found

item.title: [Solution] Tailwind CSS Build Error Fix
item.description:
Tailwind CSS Build Error

Fix Tailwind CSS build errors. Handle PostCSS configuration, purge issues, and class scanning problems..

What This Error Means

Common error scenarios include:

  • Connection or network failures
  • Invalid configuration or options
  • Resource not found or unavailable
  • Permission or access issues

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Incorrect configuration or missing dependencies
// Cause 2: Network or connection issues
// Cause 3: Invalid input or parameters
// Cause 4: Missing dependencies or resources

How to Fix

Fix 1: Verify configuration and dependencies

// Check configuration values and ensure required dependencies are installed
// Verify the module/package is properly configured for your environment

Fix 2: Add proper error handling

try {
 // Use the module/package with proper error handling
} catch (err) {
 console.error('Error:', err.message);
 // Handle gracefully
}

Fix 3: Add retry and timeout logic

// For network operations, add timeout and retry logic
// For file operations, check existence before accessing

Examples

// Common error handling pattern
try {
 // Operation that may fail
} catch (err) {
 if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
 console.error('Connection refused - check if service is running');
 } else if (err.code === 'ETIMEDOUT') {
 console.error('Operation timed out');
 }
}

Related Errors

  • ECONNREFUSED — connection refused
  • ETIMEDOUT — timeout
  • ENOENT — file not found

item.title: [Solution] Vite CSS Processing Error
item.description:
Vite CSS Processing Error

A CSS processing error occurs when Vite’s CSS pipeline fails during transformation. This may involve PostCSS, Sass, CSS modules, or the native CSS parser.

Common Causes

  • A PostCSS plugin is missing or misconfigured
  • SCSS/Less is used without the required preprocessor package
  • CSS syntax errors in the source files
  • CSS modules are used but not properly configured

How to Fix

Install Required Preprocessor

npm install --save-dev sass

Fix PostCSS Configuration

// postcss.config.js
export default {
 plugins: {
 tailwindcss: {},
 autoprefixer: {},
 },
};

Check CSS Syntax

/* WRONG: invalid syntax */
.container {
 color: red;
 background-color; /* missing colon */
}

/* CORRECT */
.container {
 color: red;
 background-color: blue;
}

Configure CSS Modules

// vite.config.ts
export default defineConfig({
 css: {
 modules: {
 localsConvention: 'camelCase',
 },
 },
});

Fix SCSS Options

export default defineConfig({
 css: {
 preprocessorOptions: {
 scss: {
 additionalData: '@use "@/styles/variables" as *;',
 },
 },
 },
});

Examples

/* Undefined variable in SCSS */
.container { color: $primary-color; }
/* ERROR: Undefined variable $primary-color */
/* Fix: import the variables file */

// PostCSS plugin not found
// ERROR: Cannot find module 'tailwindcss'
// Fix: npm install --save-dev tailwindcss

Related Errors

  • Asset Error — asset handling failure
  • Build Error — build phase failure

item.title: Vite CSS PostCSS Error
item.description:
Vite CSS — PostCSS Error

This error occurs when Vite fails to process CSS files with PostCSS or CSS preprocessors (SCSS, Sass, Less). The CSS pipeline encounters errors during compilation or transformation.

Common Causes

  • Missing PostCSS plugins
  • Incorrect PostCSS configuration
  • SCSS/Sass syntax errors
  • Missing preprocessor dependencies
  • @import paths not resolved

How to Fix

Install Required Dependencies

npm install -D postcss autoprefixer
# For SCSS
npm install -D sass

Create PostCSS Config

// postcss.config.js
module.exports = {
 plugins: [
 require('autoprefixer'),
 ],
};

Configure CSS in Vite

// vite.config.js
export default defineConfig({
 css: {
 postcss: './postcss.config.js',
 preprocessorOptions: {
 scss: {
 additionalData: `@import "./src/styles/variables.scss";`,
 },
 },
 },
});

Fix SCSS Import Paths

/* src/styles/main.scss */
@use './variables' as *; // modern Sass syntax
@import './components/button';

Fix PostCSS Plugin Order

// postcss.config.js
module.exports = {
 plugins: [
 require('postcss-import'), // must be first
 require('postcss-nested'), // second
 require('autoprefixer'), // last
 ],
};

Add Tailwind CSS Support

// postcss.config.js
module.exports = {
 plugins: {
 tailwindcss: {},
 autoprefixer: {},
 },
};

Examples

[vite] Internal server error: postcss-plugin errors:
 Syntax Error: Unexpected token at line 5 in main.scss

Related Errors

  • Vite Build Error — general build failure
  • Vite Config Error — configuration error
  • Vite React Error — React JSX errors

item.title: Vite CSS Processing Error
item.description:
Vite CSS Processing Error

A Vite CSS processing error occurs when Vite fails to process, transform, or compile CSS files. Vite uses PostCSS for CSS processing and can integrate with preprocessors like Sass, Less, and Stylus.

Common Causes

  • Missing CSS preprocessor (Sass, Less)
  • PostCSS plugin errors
  • Invalid CSS syntax
  • Missing CSS import dependencies

How to Fix

Install CSS Preprocessors

npm install --save-dev sass
# or
npm install --save-dev less

Check CSS Syntax

/* Invalid CSS */
.container {
 width: 100%
 height: 100vh
}

/* Valid CSS */
.container {
 width: 100%;
 height: 100vh;
}

Configure PostCSS

// vite.config.js
export default defineConfig({
 css: {
 postcss: {
 plugins: [
 require('autoprefixer'),
 ],
 },
 },
});

Fix CSS Import Issues

/* Ensure file exists */
@import './components/button.css';
@import 'normalize.css';

Check Preprocessor Options

export default defineConfig({
 css: {
 preprocessorOptions: {
 scss: {
 additionalData: '@import "./variables.scss";',
 },
 },
 },
});

Examples

npx vite build
[vite:css] PostCSS syntax error
file: /src/styles.scss:5:3

# Fix: correct CSS syntax or install sass
npm install --save-dev sass

Related Errors

  • Build Error — build failure
  • Plugin Error — plugin error
  • Asset Error — asset processing error

item.title: Vite Tailwind PostCSS Plugin Error
item.description:
Vite Tailwind — PostCSS Plugin Error

This error occurs when Vite fails to process Tailwind CSS due to a PostCSS plugin error. The Tailwind plugin may not be configured correctly or has a version incompatibility.

Common Causes

  • Tailwind CSS plugin not in PostCSS config
  • PostCSS plugin version mismatch
  • Invalid Tailwind configuration
  • Missing PostCSS dependencies

How to Fix

Install Required Packages

npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer
npx tailwindcss init -p

Create PostCSS Config

// postcss.config.js
export default {
 plugins: {
 tailwindcss: {},
 autoprefixer: {},
 },
};

Configure Tailwind in Vite

// vite.config.js
export default defineConfig({
 css: {
 postcss: './postcss.config.js',
 },
});

Fix Tailwind Config

// tailwind.config.js
/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
export default {
 content: [
 './index.html',
 './src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}',
 ],
 theme: {
 extend: {},
 },
 plugins: [],
};

Fix Tailwind v4 Migration

npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite

// vite.config.js
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite';

export default defineConfig({
 plugins: [tailwindcss()],
});

Examples

[vite] Internal server error:
 postcss-import: Failed to resolve "@tailwind base"
 at src/styles/main.css:1:1

Related Errors

  • Vite CSS Error — CSS processing failure
  • Vite Config Error — configuration error
  • Vite Build Error — general build failure
